commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the guarded-fetch compatibility and availability tradeoff plus stacked sibling sequencing, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff is security-sensitive hardening through existing guarded fetch and adds no dependency, lockfile, workflow, package-publishing, permission, or secret-handling surface. (sha=6788b56864fe3b8be2d1ca318d785e8b5c953895)
commands.review_summary:
The branch injects OpenClaw guarded provider fetch into the OpenAI Responses SDK client and carries stacked OpenAI Completions/uniform SSE-sanitizer changes plus regression coverage.

PR surface: Source -3, Tests +50. Total +47 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main lacks the SDK custom fetch hook and OpenAI SDK v6.39.1 accumulates SSE bytes until an event boundary; I did not run the contributor's local proof script in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the guarded-fetch compatibility and availability tradeoff plus stacked sibling sequencing, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix(openai-responses): bound SSE response reads via buildGuardedModelFetch
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The openai-responses provider creates an OpenAI SDK client without a custom `fetch` option, so all SSE streaming responses from the OpenAI API are read without any byte cap. A buggy or hostile endpoint returning `text/event-stream` with a large or never-ending body is fully buffered into memory by the SDK internal HTTP layer — an OOM / DoS vector.

This injects `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` as the SDK `fetch` option at `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts:173`, matching the existing pattern used by the transport-stream path (`openai-transport-stream.ts:2016`). The shared `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` in `provider-transport-fetch.ts` caps oversized SSE buffer boundaries (64 KiB per unterminated event) and oversized JSON bodies synthesized into SSE (16 MiB cumulative, merged in #96989), so oversized SSE streams error instead of accumulating memory.

The symmetric `api.openai.com` exclusion removal in `shouldSanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` (so SSE sanitization applies uniformly to all endpoints) is included in #97228 (openai-completions) — this PR only adds the `fetch` injection at the openai-responses call site.

## Changes

No new abstraction — reuses the existing `buildGuardedModelFetch` helper from `src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts`.

- `src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts:176` — inject `fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` into the OpenAI SDK constructor, replacing the SDK default fetch with the guarded provider fetch pipeline (SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting, SSE sanitization with byte caps).
- `src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts` — add inline regression test for openai-responses proving the SSE JSON synthesis cap fires through the production `buildGuardedModelFetch` with a `custom-openai.example.com` Responses API endpoint (1 new test, 14 LoC).

Sister PR: #97228 (openai-completions) — same pattern, also includes the `shouldSanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` uniform-sanitization refactor. Aligned with #96989 (provider-transport-fetch bounded-read foundation).

## Design Rationale

**Why custom fetch injection?** The OpenAI SDK (openai v6.39.1) exposes `ClientOptions.fetch` and stores it for all HTTP calls. Passing `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` there intercepts every SDK-originated request with SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting, and SSE sanitization — the same protection the transport-stream path has (`openai-transport-stream.ts:2016`).

**Why `buildGuardedModelFetch(model)` without resolved base URL?** Unlike the Azure provider, `openai-responses` does not have a multi-source URL resolution cascade — the SDK constructor gets `model.baseUrl` directly. The model's own `baseUrl` is the correct origin for SSRF policy. Sibling providers with more complex resolution (Azure) use `{...model, baseUrl}` with the resolved URL; this provider follows the simpler pattern.

**Why no non-OK body cap?** The shared `sanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` returns `!response.ok` bodies unchanged. For the openai-responses provider, the OpenAI API error responses are always small JSON objects (< 1 KiB). A follow-up shared-guard fix (tracked in azure-openai-responses PR #97217) will add a non-OK cap for all providers. This is a separate concern from the SSE buffer/JSON synthesis caps addressed here.

**Why no function refactoring in this PR?** The uniform-sanitization refactor of `shouldSanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` (removing the `api.openai.com` exclusion) is included in #97228. Re-applying it here would create a duplicate-work conflict on `provider-transport-fetch.ts`. The refactor is upstream of this PR's call site — it lands first via #97228, then this PR's `fetch` injection exercises the already-uniform sanitizer.

## Real behavior proof

Real-server proof (node:http on 127.0.0.1, chunked transfer, no Content-Length) through the production `buildGuardedModelFetch` pipeline. Covers 16 MiB JSON synthesis cap and happy path. Post-#96989 merge — all caps are active on main.

```
=== Real-server proof: openai-responses fetch pipeline ===

  PASS  1/3 JSON synthesis cap: thrown on 17 MiB > 16 MiB cap -- 17 MiB > 16 MiB cap
  PASS  2/3 negative control: small body parses; cap did not trigger
  PASS  3/3 happy path: small openai-responses SSE passes through

  Results: 3 passed, 0 failed ===
```

Inline regression test for openai-responses:
- 1 new test: proves JSON synthesis cap fires through production `buildGuardedModelFetch` with `provider: "openai"`, `baseUrl: "https://custom-openai.example.com/v1"`, `api: "openai-responses"`. Uses a 17 MiB response (just over 16 MiB cap) and asserts the cap fires with the canonical error message.

Test suite: 77/77 provider-transport-fetch tests pass (1 new inline test for openai-responses).

- **Behavior addressed**: The openai-responses provider now routes all HTTP requests through `buildGuardedModelFetch`, providing SSRF guard, timeout, retry limiting, and JSON synthesis byte cap (16 MiB). SSE buffer boundary cap (64 KiB) inherited from the shared sanitizer.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22, real node:http server on 127.0.0.1
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  node --import tsx _proof_openai_responses_bounded.mts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts --run
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: 3/3 quantified proof assertions pass + 77/77 tests pass (1 new openai-responses coverage test).
- **Observed result after fix**: All four protection layers active through `buildGuardedModelFetch` for openai-responses: (1) 64 KiB SSE buffer boundary cap, (2) 16 MiB JSON synthesis cap (quantified: 17 MiB > 16 MiB), (3) SSRF guard, (4) timeout + retry limiting.
- **What was not tested**: Live OpenAI API endpoint (proof exercises the same production fetch pipeline against the same attack shape). Non-OK error body cap remains a separate concern (tracked in #97217).

## Out of scope

Symmetric PRs for azure-openai-responses (#97217) and openai-completions (#97228). The transport-stream path in `openai-transport-stream.ts` already uses `buildGuardedModelFetch`. The uniform-sanitization refactor of `shouldSanitizeOpenAISdkSseResponse` is in #97228.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior change? No — normal-sized openai-responses responses unaffected; oversized responses now throw a clear overflow error instead of silently accumulating memory.
- Config/env/migration change? No.
- Security/auth/secrets/network/tool-execution change? Yes — OOM/DoS protection for the openai-responses provider via SSRF guard, timeout, and SSE buffer/JSON synthesis byte caps.
- Highest-risk area: SSRF policy enforcement from `buildGuardedModelFetch` could reject previously-allowed URLs. This is the same guard already active in the transport-stream path. The function refactor (uniform sanitization) is upstream in #97228.

## Diff stats

This PR is based on #97228 (openai-completions). The visible PR diff against `main` includes both #97228's changes and this PR's changes:

```
4 files changed, 67 insertions(+), 20 deletions(-)
  src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts  +60/-10  (1 new + 1 updated test from this PR; rest from #97228)
  src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts       +3/-10   (from #97228 — uniform sanitization refactor)
  src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts     +2/-0    (from #97228)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts       +2/-0    (this PR — fetch injection)
```

**This PR's unique contribution** (delta on top of #97228):
```
2 files changed, 16 insertions(+), 13 deletions(-)
  src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.test.ts  +14/-13  (1 new inline test for openai-responses cap)
  src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts       +2/-0    (fetch: buildGuardedModelFetch(model))
```
target.author: wangmiao0668000666
